reasoning: Libya remain politically divided between the Government of National Unity (GNU) in Tripoli and the House of Representatives (HoR) in the east. While the GNU supports African integration and participates in AU meetings, ratifying an international treaty requires legislative approval. The ongoing political gridlock makes it highly unlikely that the HoR will convene and agree to ratify the AfCFTA agreement pushed by the rival GNU before July 2025. Other conflict-affected African nations (like Sudan and South Sudan) have also stalled on ratification.
